title: impatiens taprobanica
intro: Impatiens taprobanica, also known as the Ceylon jewelweed, is a flowering plant species native to Sri Lanka, belonging to the family Balsamaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of anthraquinones and alkaloids. Consuming the plant is said to provide health benefits such as relief from skin irritations, fever reduction, and digestive issues. Impatiens taprobanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its safety and effectiveness as a medicinal treatment.
